subject: HSTS header missing
truth: Send the Strict-Transport-Security HTTP response header on all responses from HSTS-protected hosts.
quote: Strict-Transport-Security

subject: alg:none in JWT/JWS
truth: Stop using the 'none' algorithm in JWT/JWS; it bypasses all signature verification.
quote: alg:none

subject: JWT algorithm confusion
truth: Always validate that the JWT 'alg' header matches the expected algorithm; do not accept tokens with a different algorithm than intended.
quote: alg

subject: JWS/JWE algorithm registration requirements
truth: Non-authenticated encryption algorithms must be registered as 'Prohibited' for JWS or JWE use.
quote: Any identifiers registered for non-authenticated encryption algorithms or other algorithms that are otherwise unsuitable for direct use as JWS or JWE algorithms must be registered as 'Prohibited'.

subject: JWT algorithm confusion
truth: When validating a JWT, you MUST explicitly specify the expected algorithm and reject tokens that use a different algorithm; do NOT rely on the 'alg' header from the token itself.
quote: MUST explicitly specify the expected algorithm

subject: JWT algorithm none
truth: You MUST reject tokens with algorithm 'none' unless the token was explicitly expected to be unsecured; do NOT accept 'none' as a valid algorithm.
quote: MUST NOT accept tokens with algorithm 'none'

subject: JWT algorithm validation
truth: You MUST validate that the algorithm in the JWT header matches the expected algorithm before performing any cryptographic verification; do NOT trust the header blindly.
quote: MUST validate that the algorithm in the JWT header matches the expected algorithm

subject: OAuth resource owner password grant is deprecated
truth: Stop using the resource owner password credentials grant; use the authorization code grant with PKCE instead.
quote: resource owner password credentials grant

subject: PKCE is required for all OAuth authorization code flows
truth: Always use PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) with the authorization code grant, even for confidential clients.
quote: PKCE

subject: Refresh tokens must be sender-constrained or use refresh token rotation
truth: Use refresh token rotation or sender-constrained refresh tokens to prevent replay attacks.
quote: refresh token rotation

subject: SHA-1 for digital signatures
truth: Stop using SHA-1 for digital signature generation; use SHA-2 or SHA-3 family hashes instead.
quote: SHA-1 shall not be used for digital signature generation

subject: SHA-1 for verification only after 2030
truth: SHA-1 may be used for digital signature verification only through December 31, 2030; after that it is disallowed.
quote: SHA-1 may be used for digital signature verification through December 31, 2030

subject: RSA 1024-bit key length
truth: Stop using 1024-bit RSA keys for digital signatures; use at least 2048-bit RSA keys.
quote: RSA 1024-bit keys are no longer allowed for digital signatures

subject: 3DES encryption
truth: Stop using 3DES (TDEA) for encryption; use AES instead.
quote: TDEA is no longer allowed for encryption

subject: SHA-1 hash function
truth: Stop using SHA-1 for digital signatures; use SHA-2 or SHA-3 family hash functions instead.
quote: SHA-1 shall not be used for digital signature generation

subject: 3DES/TDEA block cipher
truth: Stop using 3DES/TDEA; use AES instead for symmetric encryption.
quote: TDEA is deprecated and shall not be used for new applications

subject: RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 for encryption
truth: Stop using RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 for encryption; use RSAES-OAEP instead.
quote: RSAES-PKCS1-v1_5 shall not be used for new applications

subject: RC4 stream cipher
truth: Stop using RC4; use AES-based ciphers instead.
quote: RC4 shall not be used

subject: SHA-1 for digital signatures
truth: Stop using SHA-1 for digital signature generation; use SHA-2 or SHA-3 family algorithms instead.
quote: SHA-1 for digital signatures

subject: RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 for encryption
truth: Stop using RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 for encryption; use RSAES-OAEP instead.
quote: RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 for encryption

subject: RC4 stream cipher
truth: Stop using RC4; use AES-based ciphers instead.
quote: RC4 stream cipher

subject: SHA-1 for digital signatures
truth: Stop using SHA-1 for digital signature generation; use SHA-2 or SHA-3 instead.
quote: SHA-1 shall not be used for digital signature generation

subject: RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 for encryption
truth: Stop using RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 for encryption; use RSA-OAEP instead.
quote: RSAES-PKCS1-v1_5 shall not be used for key transport

subject: 3DES encryption
truth: Stop using 3DES (TDEA); use AES instead.
quote: TDEA is deprecated and shall not be used for encryption

subject: RSA with 1024-bit keys
truth: Stop using RSA with 1024-bit keys; use 2048-bit or larger keys.
quote: RSA 1024-bit keys are deprecated and shall not be used

subject: Ephemeral key pairs vs static key pairs
truth: Do not use static key pairs for ephemeral operations; ephemeral key pairs must be generated fresh per session and their public keys must not be placed in certificates.
quote: Ephemeral key pair A short-term key pair used with a public-key (asymmetric-key) algorithm that is generated when needed ; the public key of an ephemeral key pair is not provided in a public key certificate, unlike static public keys which are often included in a certificate.

subject: key pair reuse across cryptographic purposes
truth: Do not reuse the same asymmetric key pair for both digital signatures and key establishment; generate separate key pairs for each purpose.
quote: the use of the same key pair for two different cryptographic purposes (e.g., digital signatures and key establishment) may weaken the security provided by one or both of the processes

associative: RSA key for encryption and signing
key pair for encryption and signatures
single key pair for all operations

subject: Triple-DES (3DES/TDEA) encryption
truth: Stop using 3DES/TDEA for encryption; use AES instead.
quote: the use of Triple-DES (3DES/TDEA) is deprecated

subject: Two-key Triple-DES (2TDEA) for encryption
truth: Stop using two-key Triple-DES (2TDEA) for encryption; it provides only 80 bits of security.
quote: two-key Triple-DES (2TDEA) is deprecated for encryption

subject: Three-key Triple-DES (3TDEA) for encryption
truth: Stop using three-key Triple-DES (3TDEA) for encryption after 2023; use AES instead.
quote: three-key Triple-DES (3TDEA) is deprecated for encryption

subject: RSA for key transport (key wrapping)
truth: Stop using RSA for key transport; use a key agreement scheme (e.g., FFC DH, ECC CDH) instead.
quote: RSA is not approved for key transport

subject: TLS version 1.1 and earlier
truth: Stop using TLS 1.1 and earlier; only TLS 1.2 and 1.3 are approved.
quote: TLS 1.2 is the earliest version of TLS that is approved

subject: SSLv3
truth: Stop using SSL version 3.0; it is not an approved protocol.
quote: SSL version 3.0 is not an approved protocol

subject: RC4 cipher suite
truth: Stop using RC4 cipher suites; they are not approved for TLS.
quote: RC4 cipher suites are not approved for TLS

subject: CBC mode cipher suites in TLS 1.2
truth: Stop using CBC mode cipher suites in TLS 1.2; prefer AEAD ciphers like AES-GCM.
quote: CBC mode cipher suites are not recommended for use in TLS 1.2

subject: RC4 cipher suites
truth: RC4 cipher suites shall not be used; they are broken.
quote: RC4 cipher suites shall not be used

subject: 3DES and DES cipher suites
truth: 3DES and DES cipher suites shall not be used; they are weak.
quote: 3DES and DES cipher suites shall not be used

subject: TLS version requirements
truth: Reject TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1; require TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3.
quote: TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 shall not be used

subject: SSLv3 prohibition
truth: Reject SSL 3.0; it is not an acceptable substitute for TLS.
quote: SSL 3.0 is not an acceptable substitute

subject: TLS 1.2 cipher suites
truth: Use only TLS 1.2 cipher suites that provide PFS and AEAD; reject static RSA key exchange and non-AEAD ciphers.
quote: TLS 1.2 cipher suites that provide PFS and AEAD

subject: TLS 1.3 requirement
truth: Prefer TLS 1.3 over TLS 1.2 when both endpoints support it.
quote: TLS 1.3 is preferred over TLS 1.2

subject: TLS version requirements
truth: Reject TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1; require TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3.
quote: TLS 1.2 is the minimum version

subject: CBC mode cipher suites
truth: Do not use CBC mode cipher suites with TLS 1.2; prefer AEAD ciphers like AES-GCM or AES-CCM.
quote: CBC mode cipher suites are deprecated

subject: RC4 cipher suites
truth: Do not use RC4 cipher suites; they are deprecated and insecure.
quote: RC4 cipher suites are deprecated

subject: Memorized secret complexity requirements
truth: Stop requiring composition rules (mixed character types) for memorized secrets; require minimum length of 8 characters at AAL1/AAL2 and enforce against known compromised passwords.
quote: Verifiers SHOULD NOT impose other composition rules

subject: Password composition rules
truth: Stop requiring composition rules (e.g., mixed case, digits, special characters) for memorized secrets; instead enforce minimum length and check against compromised password lists.
quote: Verifiers SHOULD NOT impose other composition rules

subject: Password hints and knowledge-based authentication
truth: Stop allowing password hints or knowledge-based authentication (e.g., mother's maiden name); verifiers SHALL NOT prompt for them.
quote: Verifiers SHALL NOT prompt subscribers to use specific types of information

associative: what is your mother's maiden name

subject: Periodic password expiration
truth: Stop requiring periodic password changes; verifiers SHOULD NOT require memorized secrets to be changed arbitrarily.
quote: Verifiers SHOULD NOT require memorized secrets to be changed arbitrarily

subject: SMS as out-of-band verifier
truth: Do not use SMS as an out-of-band authentication verifier; use a push notification to a registered device or a code from a separate software or hardware authenticator instead.
quote: SMS is not an acceptable method for out-of-band verifiers

subject: Memorized secret complexity requirements
truth: Stop requiring composition rules (mixed case, digits, special characters) for memorized secrets; require a minimum of 8 characters (AAL2) and check against known compromised passwords.
quote: Verifiers SHOULD NOT impose other composition rules

subject: Periodic password changes
truth: Do not require periodic password changes (e.g., every 90 days) unless there is evidence of compromise; only change on user request or suspected compromise.
quote: Verifiers SHOULD NOT require memorized secrets to be changed arbitrarily

subject: Knowledge-based authentication (KBA)
truth: Do not use knowledge-based authentication (e.g., mother's maiden name, security questions) as an authenticator or for identity proofing.
quote: Verifiers SHALL NOT use knowledge-based authentication

associative: what is your mother's maiden name

subject: password blacklist against breached passwords
truth: Do compare user-chosen passwords against a blacklist of unacceptable passwords including previous breach corpuses and dictionary words.
quote: it is recommended that passwords chosen by users be compared against a 'black list' of unacceptable passwords. This list should include passwords from previous breach corpuses, dictionary words
